title: Waymark Content Board
values: Your spreadsheet already IS the database. Waymark just gives it the UI it deserves. No migration, no new tool to learn — your Google Sheet stays the source of truth.
People love hearing they don't need to learn something new
I built 18 templates for Google Sheets and none of them require a single line of code. Budget trackers, kanban boards, habit trackers — all auto-detected from your column headers.
Listing specific templates drives curiosity and clicks
Showed my wife a Google Sheet she's been maintaining for 3 years. Opened it in Waymark. She said 'why didn't this exist before?' That's the reaction I'm building for.
Personal stories with real reactions crush it. Be vulnerable.

r/productivity — I made a free tool that turns Google Sheets into interactive apps. It auto-detects if your sheet is a budget, kanban board, checklist, or 15 other types and renders a proper UI. Your data stays in Sheets.
Reddit loves free tools. Lead with what it does, not what it is.

Most productivity tools want you to import your data into THEIR system. Waymark does the opposite — it meets your data where it already lives. Google Sheets is the world's most popular database whether we like it or not.
LinkedIn audience responds to contrarian takes on tool philosophy
Thread: 5 things I learned building Waymark that nobody tells you about side projects. 1/ The problem you think you're solving isn't the problem people actually have...
Show HN: Waymark — Turn your Google Sheets into kanban boards, budgets, and habit trackers. No code, no migration. Auto-detects your column headers.
Quick demo: open any Google Sheet with a 'Status' column and Waymark turns it into a kanban board in 2 seconds. No setup. No config. Just your data, made useful.
Your meal plan spreadsheet could look like this instead of a wall of cells. Waymark detects recipe columns automatically and renders a card view with categories, prep times, and ingredients.
Every teacher I know has a gradebook in Google Sheets. Waymark auto-detects student names and score columns, calculates averages, and shows letter grades. It writes back to the same sheet.
Niche use cases get less reach but more engaged comments
Blog post: How we built 18 Google Sheets templates without a single framework. Just vanilla JS, CSS custom properties, and the el() factory. Here's why.
